description: The largest collection of MCP Servers, including Awesome MCP Servers and Claude MCP integration.

primary_submission_method.title: Submit MCP Server or Client
primary_submission_method.description: Submit awesome MCP servers or clients, share with the community.

form_fields.name: type
form_fields.label: Type
form_fields.placeholder: Select the type of MCP server or client

form_fields.name: name
form_fields.label: Name
form_fields.placeholder: Input the name of the MCP server or client

form_fields.name: server_config
form_fields.label: Server Config
form_fields.description: JSON configuration for the MCP server (command, args, env)

is_dxt.label: Is DXT
is_dxt.description: Desktop Extensions: One-click local MCP server installation in desktop apps

alternative_submission_methods.description: Submit via GitHub Issues on the MCP.so repository

https://mcp.so/api/submit.note: Returns the main app page (Next.js SPA), not a dedicated API endpoint

authentication.note: Users must sign in to submit. Sign-in modal supports Email, Google, and GitHub authentication.

registration_steps: 2. Sign in using Email, Google, or GitHub authentication
3. Click 'Submit' in the sidebar navigation (or go to https://mcp.so/submit)
4. Fill in the form: Select type (Server or Client), enter Name, enter URL (typically GitHub repo URL)
5. Optionally provide Server Config JSON (command, args, env)
6. Optionally mark as Innovation or DXT
7. Click Submit to list the agent/server on MCP.so
summary: MCP.so does not expose a public REST API for registration or submission. The only official way to list an agent/server is through the web form at https://mcp.so/submit after signing in. An alternative is to open an issue on their GitHub repository at https://github.com/chatmcp/mcpso/issues. The form requires selection of type (server/client), name, and URL. Server config JSON is optional.
